gazette: Directorate General of Trade Research and Risk Assessmen
title: Sri Lanka Plans to Gradually Lift Import Restrictions on Motor Vehicles from October 1, 2024
bodyHtml:
Sri Lanka Plans to Gradually Lift Import Restrictions on Motor Vehicles from October 1, 2024

In a statement made by the Cabinet of Ministers of the Government of Sri Lanka, it was announced that the temporary ban on import of motor vehicles will be gradually lifted starting from October 1, 2024.

The import ban, imposed in March 2020, was aimed at limiting foreign exchange outflow amid the country’s deepening economic woes. With the lifting of the ban, Sri Lanka will reportedly begin to gradually import motor vehicles again as part of a broader economic recovery strategy.

According to the statement made the lifting of the temporary ban on the import of motor vehicles and non-motorized goods subject to 304 HS codes will be carried out in three stages. Accordingly:

Phase 1: Imports of public transport vehicles, special purpose vehicles and other non-motorized vehicles will be allowed from October 1, 2024.

Stage 2: The temporary ban on the import of commercial or goods vehicles will be lifted on December 1, 2024.

Stage 3: Importation of motor vehicles for personal use (cars, minibuses, sports utility vehicles, pickups, etc.) will be allowed from February 1, 2025.

In this regard, it has been stated that the gradual removal of restrictions will be useful in providing the needed momentum for the automotive sector and the general economy and in solving challenges such as the aging vehicle population, decreasing fuel efficiency and increasing maintenance costs.
